title: Illecebrum lupulinum

Illecebrum lupulinum is considered an endemic species to the Canary Islands and Cape Verde. The classification of Illecebrum lupulinum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history. Further research may be necessary to confirm the taxonomy of this species.
uses: Illecebrum lupulinum is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Western Australia, where it has been employed to treat various ailments. Its leaves are said to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help soothe skin irritations and wounds. intro: Illecebrum lupulinum, also known as lupulin, is a shrub-like plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and fragrant foliage. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Illecebrum lupulinum include essential oils, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
